_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the /format/ page. Every fileKey, enum label, and numeric range here is copied verbatim from the app's App/Core/Sources/PropertySchema.swift (the 25 Picture Profile definitions) and App/Core/Sources/ProfileIO.swift (metadata keys + parsing rules). Do NOT edit from memory. When Creative Look keys ship (app Slice 1/5), add a second group here and the table + LLM prompt both grow from it. Accuracy rule (issue #244): keys/enums/ranges must match the Swift source exactly.

keys.meaning: Black point. Lower deepens blacks (more contrast); higher lifts shadows (a flatter look).
The tone curve / dynamic-range mapping. See the gamma list below.
How much of the shadow region Black Gamma Level affects.
Lifts (+) or crushes (−) the shadows within the Black Gamma Range.
How highlight compression is decided. Auto meters the scene; Manual sets the curve by hand.
Highest signal level the auto-knee will target.
How aggressively the auto-knee tracks bright scene content.
Signal level where highlight compression begins.
Steepness of the compression curve above the knee point.
Colour space / matrix. See the colour-mode list below.
Overall colour intensity across every hue.
Rotates the hue wheel. Negative → magenta/red; positive → green.
Density of red — deepens (+) or lightens (−) reds without shifting hue.
Density of green — foliage and the green component of skin tones.
Density of blue — sky, water, and the cool side of shadows.
Density of cyan — water, sky, and the cool complement of skin.
Density of magenta — the red-blue blend in skin, florals, sunsets.
Density of yellow — foliage saturation, golden light, warm skin.
Overall in-camera sharpening. −7 softens; +7 boosts edge contrast.
Use the camera's default detail tuning (Auto) or the five manual values below.
Vertical vs. horizontal sharpening emphasis.
Sharpening emphasis on dark edges vs. bright edges.
Cap on how much sharpening any single edge can get.
Suppresses low-amplitude sharpening — keeps flat areas clean.
How much sharpening is applied within bright highlights.

metadataKeys.meaning: Profile name. The only key Lutaro needs — if omitted, the filename (without .profile) is used.
Who created the look. Optional; shown in the library.
Free-text notes. Optional. Use this for anything the source states that has no key of its own.
Comma-separated tags used to organize the library.
Marks the profile as a favourite. Optional.
A sample image shown with the profile. Give the filename of an image sitting next to the .profile file — not a path.
